Una gran amiga de todos los hispanos aquí en West Monroe y Monroe ya sospecha que tiene la enfermedad COVID-19. 
Ella explica aqui que no hay suficientes exámenes disponibles para esa enfermedad y por eso no pueden examinar a ella y a sus hijas.
Oremos por Amber Amador y PORFAVOR tengan muchisimo cuidado de no contactar mucho a otros y de mantener distancia durante estos dias. 
NOSOTROS podemos salvar las vidas de otros por simplemente obedecer las precauciones que nos dan en el gobierno.

We recognize the need for essential business in our area so we decided to open up with limited hours thru April 14th. Bare with us as we are having issues with our suppliers during this hard time but we are here for you!

posts.text: Ya esta la comida recién hecha, con el sabor único que nos distingue  platillos para el día de hoy  1) ASADO DE PUERCO 2) POLLO EN MOLE  3) BISTEK ENCEBOLLADO vengan a visitarnos a darle gusto al paladar con nuestros ya tradicionales tacos de maiz y harina, gorditas, pupusas, entomatadas, enchiladas acompañadas de nuestras deliciosas aguas frescas de Horchata y Jamaica, coca cola mexicana de vidrio, servicio de envios trato cordial y con la tasa de cambio mas alta en la localidad, nuestra tienda esta completamente surtida para estos tiempos dificiles mejores precios para tus necesidades proximamente tendremos pan calientito recién hecho. Nuestro servicio de carnicería se reanuda hoy carne fresca estará lista para esta tarde vengan por su fajita marinada,  barbacoa, costilla chorizo etc los esperamos raza...
¿Algunos de ustedes de Honduras conocen a este pastor Carlos Lopez de Honduras?

results.sum: Mario Mata selling freshly made tamales starting at 11am at 222 Blanchard St, West Monroe (in front of cinema) — order through the window.

Mirna Melissa Clatza looking for someone to clean leaves and pine straw from yard — prefers someone out of work due to restaurant closures, call 318-503-0981; commenter Leonor Mata offers service and provides 318-608-0895.
Mario Mata selling fresh pork and chicken tamales at 222 Blanchard St, West Monroe — $8 for half dozen, $15 for dozen, $27 for 2 dozen; order through window.
Carlos Vides López selling pupusas tomorrow — $10/order with soda included, home delivery available — call 318-805-5404.
Medina Market staying open during COVID, maintaining same prices — call 318-654-4291 to place orders for pickup.
Michael Strickland shares information about LA shelter-in-place restrictions — explains which activities are legal vs. illegal under Los Angeles rules, noting similar rules may soon apply nationwide.
Michael Strickland posts an image (no text); comments respond with 'Amen' — likely a religious or inspirational image shared during the COVID period.
Carlos Vides López announces pupusas are ready to order — call the number in the post (318-805-5404).
Centro Compasion posts with no text content; single commenter responds 'Amen' — likely a shared religious content post.
Mario Mata selling pork, chicken, and picadillo tamales at 222 Blanchard St, West Monroe — order through window.
Lorena Ayala announces fresh bread has arrived at 107 New Natchitoches Rd, West Monroe.
Mario Mata at 222 Blanchard St, West Monroe selling avocado tortas, carnitas tortas, and pork/chicken/picadillo tamales — order through window.
COVID-19 alert: confirmed cases are now in the Monroe/West Monroe area — urges community to stay home, avoid visits, and take extreme precautions.
Suli Del Rosal informs parents of First Communion students that home-study materials prepared by Mrs. Kathy Wallace are available for pickup today 12–2pm at the Sunday school.
Michael Strickland shares a Spanish-language COVID-19 symptom video; refers to Amber Amador's experience and asks community to pray for all those affected.
Tienda Eben-Ezer at 107 New Natchitoches Rd, West Monroe will be open Monday–Sunday 8am–5pm during the COVID period.
COVID update: Louisiana Governor orders shelter-in-place starting tomorrow at 5pm for two weeks — only essential activities allowed.
Wilson Guzmán posts a humorous bilingual note during COVID lockdown: 'Dear taco trucks, please cruise the neighborhoods like ice cream trucks. Sincerely, Latinos West Monroe.'
Centro Compasion announces an online religious service — 'If you missed this service, connect today.'
Frank Lushito posts an image with no text; all 9 commenters respond with 'Amen' — likely a religious or inspirational image.
Chase Turner announces a Spanish Bible study tomorrow (Wednesday) at 7pm on the topic of jobs and anxiety.
Michael Strickland announces that local community friend Amber Amador suspects she has COVID-19 but can't get tested; asks community to pray for her and maintain social distancing.
Michael Strickland (duplicate post) about Amber Amador's suspected COVID-19 illness; urges prayers and social distancing — insufficient testing available.
Medina Market/Latin Food & Cafeteria reopening with limited hours through April 14 as an essential business — limited supplies but open for the community.
Latin Food & Cafeteria page shares: Medina Market reopening with limited hours through April 14 as essential business during COVID-19 — limited supplies available.
Jen Medina posts 4 sun emojis with 3 store images — likely announcing that the market is open for the day.
Lorena Ayala announces fresh bread has arrived at 107 New Natchitoches Rd, West Monroe (zip 71292).
Naty Lopez selling tres leches cakes — call 318-334-3719 in Monroe.
Chase Turner invites community to an online bilingual service Sunday at 1pm — Lord's Supper followed by a short lesson on prayer.
Jay C Nuñez shares an encouraging COVID message: together with God, extreme cleanliness and protection we will overcome this terrible virus.
Chase Turner shares a video Bible study on finances and anxiety — invites the community to watch.
Michael Strickland recommends a good tire shop on Washington St, West Monroe — they understand Spanish perfectly, do great tire repair, and also sell used tires.
Lorena Ayala announces fresh bread has arrived at 107 New Natchitoches Rd, West Monroe.
Centro Compasion posts with no text but receives a religious blessing comment — likely a shared religious image or video.
Naty Lopez selling tres leches cakes at $25 each in Monroe — call 318-334-3719.
Lupita Morato asking where to find palm leaves for Palm Sunday.
Michael Strickland posts an image (no text); comments respond with 'Amen' — likely a religious or COVID-encouragement image.
Wilson Guzmán looking for ZOTE soap in West Monroe; commenters direct him to Mexican stores, Tienda Doña Mary, Walmart, and Super 1.
Chase Turner announces an online Sunday service starting at 1:15.
Crystal Berumen looking for someone who does car detailing.
Guadalupe González posts 'hispanos en West Monroe y Monroe Louisiana' with a commenter responding 'Amen' — appears to be a community prayer or shoutout post.
Gabriel Hernandez shares a Facebook link about the increase in COVID-19 cases in the area — urges caution.
Angelica Villaverde posts an image (no text); commenter responds with a COVID prayer for family health and for the world virus to disappear.

Jay C Nuñez announces full menu available: asado de puerco, pollo en mole, bistec encebollado; tacos, gorditas, pupusas, enchiladas, aguas frescas (horchata, jamaica), Mexican Coke; also delivery, butcher service, and money exchange — fully stocked store.
Michael Strickland asks if anyone knows Honduran pastor Carlos Lopez — commenter warns the shared COVID herbal cure claim is false misinformation.
Chase Turner shares Bible study blessing videos for the community to watch.
Michael Strickland shares a store image (no text); comment notes Medina Market has Masecas and fresh bolillos — invites community to shop there.
Centro Compasion invites community members to submit prayer requests — 'We will be praying, post your prayer request and God will hear my prayers.'

results.svc: Latin grocery and seafood for Holy Week
results.sum: Tienda y Carnicería Doña Mary supporting Holy Week with seafood stew (caldo de mariscos) and fried tilapia — come visit.
Tienda y Carnicería Doña Mary also offering fish tacos for Holy Week.
